2026-04-22T08:37:44.6631884Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T08:37:44.6632437Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T08:37:44.6632865Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T08:37:44.6633229Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T08:37:44.6633547Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T08:37:44.6633788Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T08:37:44.6633959Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T08:37:44.6634150Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T08:37:44.6634326Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T08:37:44.6634565Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-22T08:37:44.6635059Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T08:37:44.6635323Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. The fix commit (4cb62dad) corrected the CHANGELOG entry for this release. The package is now on a new preview API version but the README has no service-specific documentation to accompany it.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **No service-specific Key concepts section** — the current \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section only links out to a generic ARM SDK introduction; there is no description of the `ComputeFleet` resource model, its properties (e.g., VM profiles, spot priority, capacity targets), or how the fleet orchestration works\\n- **No code examples in the README** — the \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section just points to `(aka.ms/redacted) there are no inline snippets showing how to create, list, or delete a fleet, nor how to configure spot/on-demand VM profiles\\n- **No `Samples/` directory** — `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/tests/` has no `Samples/` subfolder, so there are no test-backed code snippets available for the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling to inject into the README\\n- **New API version (2025-07-01-preview) not described** — the CHANGELOG notes the API upgrade under \\u0026#34;Features Added\\u0026#34; but the README does not describe what new capabilities are available in this preview API version\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the generic \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section with service-specific content, and replace the \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section with a snippet placeholder. The updated sections should look like this:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n**Azure Compute Fleet** lets you deploy and manage a combination of Standard, Spot, and On-demand virtual machines within a single fleet resource (`ComputeFleetResource`). Key concepts include:\\n\\n- **Fleet**: A top-level ARM resource (`Microsoft.AzureFleet/fleets`) that declares the VM profiles, capacity targets, and compute preferences for the fleet.\\n- **VM profiles**: One or more `ComputeFleetVmProfile` entries that describe the VM sizes, images, and disk configurations to use.\\n- **Spot priority**: Configure `SpotPriorityProfile` to set maximum price, capacity, and eviction policies for Spot VMs.\\n- **On-demand priority**: Configure `OnDemandCapacityReservationProfile` to set the on-demand VM capacity target.\\n\\nFor the complete type reference see the [API References]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Compute Fleet\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List Compute Fleets in a resource group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file at `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/tests/Samples/SampleComputeFleet.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class SampleComputeFleet : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateFleet()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeFleet_CreateFleet\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ComputeFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetComputeFleets();\\n\\n            var fleetData = new ComputeFleetData(AzureLocation.EastUS)\\n            {\\n                Properties = new ComputeFleetProperties\\n                {\\n                    SpotPriorityProfile = new SpotPriorityProfile\\n                    {\\n                        Capacity = 20,\\n                        MinCapacity = 10,\\n                        MaxPricePerVM = -1,\\n                        EvictionPolicy = SpotEvictionPolicy.Delete,\\n                        AllocationStrategy = SpotAllocationStrategy.PriceCapacityOptimized,\\n                    },\\n                    OnDemandCapacityReservationProfile = new OnDemandCapacityReservationProfile\\n                    {\\n                        CapacityReservationGroupId = null,\\n                    },\\n                    ComputeProfile = new ComputeFleetComputeProfile(\\n                        new System.Collections.Generic.List\\n                        {\\n                            new ComputeFleetVmProfile\\n                            {\\n                                VmSize = \\u0026#34;Standard_D2s_v3\\u0026#34;,\\n                                Count = 5,\\n                            },\\n                        }),\\n                },\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, fleetData);\\n            ComputeFleetResource fleet = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Fleet created: {fleet.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListFleets()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeFleet_ListFleets\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (ComputeFleetResource fleet in resourceGroup.GetComputeFleets())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Fleet: {fleet.Data.Name} in {fleet.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor each snippet:\\n- `ComputeFleet_CreateFleet` — maps to the `#region Snippet:ComputeFleet_CreateFleet` block above; add placeholder in README under \\u0026#34;### Create a Compute Fleet\\u0026#34;\\n- `ComputeFleet_ListFleets` — maps to the `#region Snippet:ComputeFleet_ListFleets` block above; add placeholder in README under \\u0026#34;### List Compute Fleets in a resource group\\u0026#34;\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Adjust the exact model constructor signatures to match the generated types in `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/src/Generated/`. Check the `ComputeFleetData`, `ComputeFleetProperties`, `SpotPriorityProfile`, and `ComputeFleetComputeProfile` types before finalizing the sample.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/README.md` currently contains all required sections but they need updating:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action |\\n|---------|--------|--------|\\n| Getting started — Install the package | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started — Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started — Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic link only | Replace with service-specific content (see Step 1) |\\n| Examples | ⚠️ Generic link only | Add snippet placeholders (see Step 1) |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 computefleet` — injects the `#region Snippet:` code from the new test file into the README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README was updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 computefleet` — regenerates API listing files (no new public surface expected, but run to ensure consistency)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/` — ensures consistent formatting\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class SampleComputeFleet : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ComputeFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetComputeFleets();\\n\\n            var fleetData = new ComputeFleetData(AzureLocation.EastUS)\\n            {\\n                Properties = new ComputeFleetProperties\\n                {\\n                    SpotPriorityProfile = new SpotPriorityProfile\\n                    {\\n                        Capacity = 20,\\n                        MinCapacity = 10,\\n                        MaxPricePerVM = -1,\\n ComputeFleetVmProfile\\n                            {\\n                                VmSize = \\u0026#34;Standard_D2s_v3\\u0026#34;,\\n                                Count = 5,\\n                            },\\n                        }),\\n                },\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, fleetData);\\n            ComputeFleetResource fleet = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Fleet created: {fleet.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await
